name: Flanders (Belgium)

colors.symbolism: the gold of the heraldic field

description: A gold field charged centrally with the black Flemish Lion (Vlaamse Leeuw), armed and tongued red.
history: The Vlaamse Leeuw is the historic emblem of the County of Flanders, attested from the 12th century. Adopted as the official flag of the modern Flemish Region by decree on 11 July 1973.

features.role: Flemish Lion (Vlaamse Leeuw), black lion rampant with red tongue and claws
features.symbolism: the historic arms of the County of Flanders, attested from the 12th century
